properties.text: For all inputs to cell_to_coset_evals (SG-001), every byte segment cell[i*BYTES_PER_FIELD_ELEMENT : (i+1)*BYTES_PER_FIELD_ELEMENT] MUST represent a value strictly less than BLS_MODULUS. If bytes_to_bls_field receives a byte segment >= BLS_MODULUS, it MUST reject with an error, never silently reduce modulo BLS_MODULUS.

reachability.notes: An attacker can submit a blob via P2P containing byte sequences that encode values >= BLS_MODULUS. If bytes_to_bls_field silently wraps around, different client implementations may produce different field elements from the same blob, causing consensus divergence.

properties.severity_justification: Silent modular reduction vs. rejection across clients would produce different polynomial commitments and proofs from identical blobs, causing a consensus split across the entire network.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: Requires PoC blob with a field element encoding >= BLS_MODULUS that produces different behavior in two or more client implementations.

properties.text: cell_to_coset_evals (SG-001) and coset_evals_to_cell (SG-002) MUST be exact inverses: for all valid CosetEvals c, cell_to_coset_evals(coset_evals_to_cell(c)) == c, and for all valid Cells c', coset_evals_to_cell(cell_to_coset_evals(c')) == c'.

reachability.notes: Round-trip encoding/decoding failures would corrupt cell data along the entire blob processing pipeline. Attacker-submitted blobs traverse this path.

properties.severity_justification: Non-invertible serialization corrupts all downstream polynomial evaluations, KZG proofs, and data availability guarantees. Different round-trip behavior across clients causes consensus divergence.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: Requires a field element value that round-trips to a different value in at least one client.

properties.text: The output of cell_to_coset_evals (SG-001) MUST contain exactly FIELD_ELEMENTS_PER_CELL elements. No more, no fewer, regardless of cell content.

reachability.notes: Cells received over P2P from peers could have unexpected sizes if validation is missing. Incorrect element count would cause FFT dimension mismatches.

properties.severity_justification: Incorrect element count would cause crashes or undefined behavior in FFT operations that require power-of-2 sized inputs.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: Requires crafted cell that produces wrong element count in a specific client.

properties.text: The output of coset_evals_to_cell (SG-002) MUST produce a Cell of exactly FIELD_ELEMENTS_PER_CELL * BYTES_PER_FIELD_ELEMENT bytes.

reachability.notes: Output size is determined by internal constants. A violation would indicate an implementation bug rather than an external attack.

properties.severity_justification: Incorrect cell size would cause downstream serialization failures but requires an implementation bug to trigger.

properties.text: _fft_field (SG-003) MUST only accept inputs where len(vals) is a power of 2. If len(vals) is not a power of 2, the function MUST reject the input before performing any computation.

reachability.notes: If an attacker can influence the size of data reaching FFT (e.g., through malformed cells that bypass size validation), non-power-of-2 inputs would cause infinite recursion or incorrect splits.

properties.severity_justification: Non-power-of-2 input to recursive FFT causes infinite recursion (stack overflow crash) or incorrect recursive splits producing wrong results.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: Requires PoC showing a code path where external input reaches FFT with non-power-of-2 length.

properties.text: _fft_field (SG-003) requires len(vals) == len(roots_of_unity). A mismatch MUST be rejected. This invariant must hold at every recursive call level, not just the top-level entry.

reachability.notes: Roots of unity are computed from protocol constants. Mismatch would require an implementation error in compute_roots_of_unity.

properties.severity_justification: Mismatch between vals and roots_of_unity lengths would produce algebraically incorrect FFT results, silently corrupting all downstream polynomial operations.

properties.text: When inv=True, fft_field (SG-004) MUST apply the normalization factor 1/N (where N = len(vals)) to each output element. The inverse FFT followed by forward FFT MUST be the identity transformation: fft(ifft(vals)) == vals.

reachability.notes: Inverse FFT is used in polynomial_eval_to_coeff (SG-007) which processes attacker-submitted blob data. Missing normalization would produce incorrect polynomial coefficients.

properties.severity_justification: Missing 1/N normalization in inverse FFT would produce incorrect polynomial coefficients for every blob, causing all KZG proofs to be invalid and breaking data availability across the network.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: Requires demonstrating a client that omits or incorrectly computes the normalization factor.

properties.text: coset_fft_field (SG-005) with shift_factor = PRIMITIVE_ROOT_OF_UNITY MUST evaluate polynomials on a coset that does not intersect the FFT evaluation domain. Specifically, for all x in the coset domain and all y in the FFT domain, x != y. This guarantees that division by the vanishing polynomial in recovery (SG-023) never encounters division by zero.

reachability.notes: An attacker providing partial cells for recovery triggers coset FFT evaluation. If the coset intersects the FFT domain, division by zero occurs during recovery, crashing the node.

properties.severity_justification: Division by zero during polynomial recovery would crash the node, preventing it from recovering data for data availability. If PRIMITIVE_ROOT_OF_UNITY is incorrectly chosen, all recovery operations network-wide would fail.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: Requires demonstrating that PRIMITIVE_ROOT_OF_UNITY choice in a specific client leads to coset-FFT domain intersection.

properties.text: The Fiat-Shamir challenge r computed by compute_verify_cell_kzg_proof_batch_challenge (SG-006) MUST include ALL verifier-relevant data in the hash input: domain separator, FIELD_ELEMENTS_PER_BLOB, FIELD_ELEMENTS_PER_CELL, len(commitments), len(cell_indices), all commitments, all commitment_indices, all cell_indices, all coset evaluations, and all proofs.

reachability.notes: An attacker submitting cells and proofs for batch verification controls all hash inputs. If any verifier-relevant field is omitted from the hash, the attacker can substitute invalid data that passes verification.

properties.severity_justification: Omitting any field from the Fiat-Shamir hash input allows an attacker to craft inputs where invalid proofs pass batch verification, completely breaking data availability guarantees.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: Requires identifying a client implementation that omits a field from the Fiat-Shamir hash computation.

properties.text: The domain separator RANDOM_CHALLENGE_KZG_CELL_BATCH_DOMAIN used in compute_verify_cell_kzg_proof_batch_challenge (SG-006) MUST be globally unique across all Ethereum protocols using the same hash function. No other protocol context may use the same domain separator value.

reachability.notes: Cross-protocol replay attacks become possible if the domain separator collides with another protocol. An attacker could replay a valid Fiat-Shamir challenge from one context in another.

properties.severity_justification: Domain separator collision enables cross-protocol Fiat-Shamir replay attacks, potentially allowing invalid proofs to pass verification by reusing challenges from a different protocol context.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: Requires identifying a domain separator collision with another deployed protocol.

properties.text: The hash input order in compute_verify_cell_kzg_proof_batch_challenge (SG-006) MUST be deterministic and canonical across all client implementations. All clients MUST serialize fields in the identical order: domain, FIELD_ELEMENTS_PER_BLOB, FIELD_ELEMENTS_PER_CELL, len(commitments), len(cell_indices), commitments, then for each k: commitment_indices[k], cell_indices[k], coset_evals[k], proofs[k].

reachability.notes: Different serialization order would produce different challenge r values across clients, causing batch verification to produce different results.

properties.severity_justification: Non-deterministic hash input ordering across clients produces different Fiat-Shamir challenges, causing consensus divergence on batch verification results for identical inputs.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: Requires demonstrating two clients that serialize the Fiat-Shamir hash input in different orders.

properties.text: polynomial_eval_to_coeff (SG-007) MUST accept exactly FIELD_ELEMENTS_PER_BLOB evaluations as input. Inputs of any other length MUST be rejected.

reachability.notes: The number of evaluations is derived from blob size. If blob_to_polynomial produces a non-standard number of evaluations, this invariant would be violated.

properties.severity_justification: Incorrect evaluation count would produce polynomials of wrong degree, causing all subsequent KZG proof operations to be algebraically invalid.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: Requires a code path where blob_to_polynomial returns non-FIELD_ELEMENTS_PER_BLOB evaluations.

properties.text: polynomial_eval_to_coeff (SG-007) MUST apply bit_reversal_permutation BEFORE the inverse FFT. Omitting or reordering this step produces incorrect coefficient representations that silently corrupt all KZG proofs.

reachability.notes: Every blob submitted triggers polynomial_eval_to_coeff. Incorrect bit-reversal ordering would produce wrong coefficients for all blobs processed by the affected client.

properties.severity_justification: Bit-reversal permutation inconsistency between encoding (SG-007) and decoding (SG-023 recovery) paths causes all proofs generated by one client to fail verification on another, splitting the network.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: Requires identifying a client where bit-reversal is applied in the wrong order or omitted.

properties.text: add_polynomialcoeff (SG-008) MUST produce a result polynomial whose degree equals max(deg(a), deg(b)). The shorter polynomial MUST be zero-padded for missing coefficients, not truncated.

reachability.notes: Polynomial addition is a pure internal operation. Incorrectness would be an implementation bug.

properties.severity_justification: Incorrect polynomial addition would propagate errors to interpolation and proof generation but requires an implementation bug to trigger.

properties.text: multiply_polynomialcoeff (SG-009) MUST assert that len(a) + len(b) <= FIELD_ELEMENTS_PER_EXT_BLOB before computing the product. The product polynomial degree MUST equal deg(a) + deg(b).

reachability.notes: During recovery (SG-023), attacker-supplied cells determine the polynomial that is multiplied. If the degree bound assertion is missing, polynomials could exceed the trusted setup bounds.

properties.severity_justification: Exceeding FIELD_ELEMENTS_PER_EXT_BLOB degree bound would produce polynomials outside the trusted setup support, causing commitments to be computed with insufficient setup points, leading to incorrect or exploitable proofs.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: Requires demonstrating a recovery scenario where the product polynomial exceeds the degree bound.

properties.text: divide_polynomialcoeff (SG-010) MUST NOT accept a zero polynomial as the divisor b. If b is the zero polynomial, the function MUST reject the input with an error, never returning a result.

reachability.notes: The divisor is typically a vanishing polynomial computed from cell indices. A zero divisor would require all cell indices to be empty, which is blocked by the 50% threshold check.

properties.severity_justification: Division by zero polynomial would cause a runtime crash. The 50% cell availability threshold should prevent this, but a missing check could lead to node crash.

properties.text: divide_polynomialcoeff (SG-010) MUST ensure the leading coefficient of the divisor b[bpos] is nonzero. Division by a polynomial with a zero leading coefficient produces incorrect quotients.

reachability.notes: Vanishing polynomials are monic (leading coefficient 1) by construction, so this is a defensive check.

properties.severity_justification: Monic vanishing polynomials guarantee a nonzero leading coefficient. Violation would require a bug in vanishing_polynomialcoeff.

properties.text: interpolate_polynomialcoeff (SG-011) MUST require all x-values (xs) to be distinct. Duplicate x-values cause division by zero in the weight_adjustment computation (xs[i] - xs[j]).inverse(), which MUST crash or be explicitly rejected.

reachability.notes: During batch verification (SG-015), interpolation is performed over coset points derived from attacker-supplied cell_indices. If duplicate cell_indices are not rejected, duplicate x-values would reach interpolation.

properties.severity_justification: Division by zero in field element inverse computation would crash the verifier node, causing denial of service. Alternatively, some implementations might return zero for a zero inverse, producing silently incorrect results.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: Requires PoC with duplicate cell_indices that bypass deduplication and reach interpolation.

properties.text: For interpolate_polynomialcoeff (SG-011), the result polynomial MUST satisfy evaluate(result, xs[i]) == ys[i] for all i. The result degree MUST be at most len(xs) - 1.

reachability.notes: Lagrange interpolation correctness is a mathematical invariant. Violation indicates an implementation bug.

properties.severity_justification: Incorrect interpolation would produce wrong commitment weights in batch verification, but requires an implementation bug.

properties.text: vanishing_polynomialcoeff (SG-012) MUST produce a monic polynomial (leading coefficient 1) of degree exactly len(xs) that evaluates to 0 at every point in xs.

reachability.notes: Vanishing polynomial construction is algebraically determined. Violation would require a multiplication bug.

properties.severity_justification: Incorrect vanishing polynomial would produce wrong quotient polynomials in KZG proof generation, corrupting proofs.

properties.text: evaluate_polynomialcoeff (SG-013) MUST implement Horner's method correctly such that the result equals sum(coeff[i] * z^i) for all i. The evaluation MUST be computed in O(n) time using the recurrence y = y * z + coeff[i] applied in reverse coefficient order.

reachability.notes: Polynomial evaluation is used in KZG proof generation (SG-014) and cell computation (SG-018). Incorrectness would produce wrong evaluations.

properties.severity_justification: Incorrect Horner evaluation would produce wrong cell evaluations and quotient polynomials, corrupting proofs.

properties.text: compute_kzg_proof_multi_impl (SG-014) MUST compute the quotient polynomial Q(X) = (f(X) - I(X)) / Z(X) where I(X) interpolates f at the evaluation points and Z(X) is the vanishing polynomial over those points. The proof MUST be a commitment to Q(X) using KZG_SETUP_G1_MONOMIAL.

reachability.notes: KZG proof generation is triggered for every blob submitted. The proof must be correctly computed for the data availability guarantee to hold.

properties.severity_justification: Incorrect KZG proof generation would produce proofs that either (1) fail verification on honest nodes causing consensus split, or (2) pass verification for incorrect data, breaking data availability.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: Requires demonstrating incorrect quotient polynomial computation in a client implementation.

properties.text: The quotient polynomial in compute_kzg_proof_multi_impl (SG-014) MUST have degree deg(f) - len(zs). If the quotient degree exceeds the trusted setup bound (len(KZG_SETUP_G1_MONOMIAL) - 1), proof generation MUST fail, never silently producing an invalid commitment.

reachability.notes: If recovery (SG-023) produces a higher-degree polynomial than expected, the quotient polynomial could exceed the trusted setup bounds.

properties.severity_justification: Exceeding the trusted setup degree bound means the g1_lincomb commitment is computed with insufficient setup elements, producing an invalid or unpredictable commitment.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: Requires demonstrating a recovery path that produces a polynomial exceeding the setup degree bound.

properties.text: verify_cell_kzg_proof_batch_impl (SG-015) MUST verify the pairing equation e(LL, LR) == e(RL, [1]) where LL = g1_lincomb(proofs, r_powers), LR = KZG_SETUP_G2_MONOMIAL[n], and RL = RLC - RLI + RLP. The pairing check MUST return True only when the KZG polynomial commitment relationship holds for ALL cells in the batch.

reachability.notes: Batch verification is the primary cryptographic gatekeeper. An attacker submits cells, commitments, and proofs for verification. Any error in the verification equation allows forged data to be accepted.

properties.severity_justification: A broken verification equation would allow an attacker to forge valid-looking cell proofs for arbitrary data, completely undermining data availability guarantees and potentially enabling invalid state transitions.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: Requires PoC demonstrating forged proofs that pass batch verification due to an implementation error in the pairing equation.

properties.text: verify_cell_kzg_proof_batch_impl (SG-015) MUST assert that commitment_indices list has no duplicate commitment entries AND that all commitment_indices reference valid entries in the commitments list (commitment_indices[k] < len(commitments) for all k).

reachability.notes: An attacker could submit out-of-bounds commitment_indices to cause array index out-of-bounds access, or duplicate commitments to manipulate the weight aggregation.

properties.severity_justification: Out-of-bounds commitment_indices would cause a crash (DoS). Duplicate commitments could cause incorrect weight aggregation in the random linear combination, potentially allowing invalid proofs to pass.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: Requires demonstrating a client that does not validate commitment_indices bounds or deduplication.

properties.text: coset_shift_for_cell (SG-016) MUST assert cell_index < CELLS_PER_EXT_BLOB. Any cell_index >= CELLS_PER_EXT_BLOB MUST be rejected. The returned value MUST be a root of unity from the bit-reversal-permuted extended domain.

reachability.notes: Cell indices provided by an attacker for batch verification (SG-021) flow into coset_shift_for_cell. Out-of-bounds indices would access memory beyond the roots array.

properties.severity_justification: Out-of-bounds cell_index causes array out-of-bounds access, potentially crashing the node or reading adjacent memory.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: Requires PoC with out-of-bounds cell_index that bypasses validation and reaches coset_shift_for_cell.

properties.text: coset_for_cell (SG-017) MUST return exactly FIELD_ELEMENTS_PER_CELL elements for any valid cell_index. The coset elements MUST form the set {h, h*g, h*g^2, ...} where h = coset_shift_for_cell(cell_index) and g is a primitive root of unity of order FIELD_ELEMENTS_PER_CELL.

reachability.notes: Coset computation is deterministic from constants. Incorrectness requires a bug in root-of-unity computation.

properties.severity_justification: Incorrect coset generation would produce wrong evaluation points for all cells, causing all proofs to be algebraically invalid.

properties.text: compute_cells (SG-018) MUST assert len(blob) == BYTES_PER_BLOB and MUST produce exactly CELLS_PER_EXT_BLOB cells. Each cell MUST represent the polynomial evaluation over a distinct coset of the extended domain.

reachability.notes: Blobs submitted via P2P or transactions are processed by compute_cells. Missing blob length validation would allow processing of malformed blobs.

properties.severity_justification: Missing blob size assertion allows processing blobs of arbitrary size, producing incorrect cell counts that cause downstream failures in sidecar construction, proof generation, and verification.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: Requires PoC with a blob of non-standard size that bypasses validation in a client.

properties.text: compute_cells_and_kzg_proofs_polynomialcoeff (SG-019) MUST produce exactly CELLS_PER_EXT_BLOB cells and proofs. Each proof MUST be valid for its corresponding cell and coset, verifiable by verify_cell_kzg_proof_batch.

reachability.notes: This is the core proof generation function invoked for every blob. A cell-proof misalignment would cause all sidecars for that blob to be rejected.

properties.severity_justification: Cell-proof misalignment causes all sidecars for the affected blob to fail verification, effectively making the blob's data unavailable despite being correctly submitted.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: Requires demonstrating a blob that produces misaligned cell-proof pairs in a specific client.

properties.text: compute_cells_and_kzg_proofs (SG-020) MUST assert len(blob) == BYTES_PER_BLOB before any processing. The function MUST produce identical results to manually calling blob_to_polynomial, polynomial_eval_to_coeff, and compute_cells_and_kzg_proofs_polynomialcoeff in sequence.

reachability.notes: This is the public entry point for blob-to-cell conversion. Missing validation would allow arbitrary-size blobs to be processed.

properties.severity_justification: Missing blob size validation at the public entry point allows malformed blobs to enter the cryptographic pipeline.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: Requires demonstrating a client that does not validate blob size in this public method.

properties.text: verify_cell_kzg_proof_batch (SG-021) MUST validate ALL of the following before proceeding to cryptographic verification: (1) all four input lists have equal length, (2) each commitment has exactly BYTES_PER_COMMITMENT bytes, (3) each cell_index < CELLS_PER_EXT_BLOB, (4) each cell has exactly BYTES_PER_CELL bytes, (5) each proof has exactly BYTES_PER_PROOF bytes.

reachability.notes: This is the public verification entry point. All inputs come from untrusted peers over P2P. Missing any validation check could lead to crashes, buffer overflows, or incorrect verification results.

properties.severity_justification: Missing input validation on the public verification entry point allows an attacker to submit malformed data that crashes nodes or bypasses verification, potentially enabling data withholding attacks.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: Requires PoC with malformed input that bypasses validation in a client implementation.

properties.text: verify_cell_kzg_proof_batch (SG-021) commitment deduplication MUST preserve semantic equivalence: after deduplication, the verification result MUST be identical to verifying each cell against its original (non-deduplicated) commitment.

reachability.notes: An attacker could submit duplicate commitments with different cell data to test whether deduplication preserves correct index mapping.

properties.severity_justification: Incorrect deduplication could map cells to the wrong commitments, causing valid proofs to be rejected or invalid proofs to be accepted.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: Requires demonstrating a deduplication implementation that incorrectly maps commitment indices.

properties.text: construct_vanishing_polynomial (SG-022) MUST produce a polynomial that evaluates to 0 at all missing cell evaluation points and is nonzero at all present cell evaluation points. The coefficient spacing by FIELD_ELEMENTS_PER_CELL MUST correctly extend the polynomial to the full extended domain.

reachability.notes: The set of missing cells is determined by which cells an attacker chooses not to provide during recovery. Incorrect vanishing polynomial construction would corrupt recovery.

properties.severity_justification: Incorrect vanishing polynomial would cause recovery to produce wrong polynomials, generating invalid cells and proofs that fail verification against the original commitment.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: Requires demonstrating a specific set of missing cell indices that triggers incorrect vanishing polynomial construction.

properties.text: recover_polynomialcoeff (SG-023) MUST satisfy the algebraic identity E(x) * Z(x) == P(x) * Z(x) over the FFT domain, where E is the extended evaluation, Z is the vanishing polynomial, and P is the recovered polynomial. The division by Z(x) MUST be performed over a coset to avoid division by zero.

reachability.notes: An attacker providing partial cells for recovery controls which cells are available and their content. Inconsistent cells (from different polynomials) would produce an incorrect recovered polynomial.

properties.severity_justification: Incorrect polynomial recovery produces wrong cells and proofs that fail verification, preventing data recovery and potentially causing nodes to disagree on data availability.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: Requires PoC with inconsistent cells that cause recovery to produce a different polynomial than the original.

properties.text: recover_polynomialcoeff (SG-023) MUST truncate the recovered polynomial to degree < FIELD_ELEMENTS_PER_BLOB by returning only poly_coeff[:FIELD_ELEMENTS_PER_BLOB]. Coefficients beyond this index (in the extension domain) MUST be zero for a correctly recovered polynomial.

reachability.notes: If an attacker provides mutually inconsistent cells, the recovered polynomial may have non-zero high-degree coefficients. Missing truncation or missing validation of high-degree zeroes could produce a polynomial outside the original blob domain.

properties.severity_justification: If high-degree coefficients are nonzero after recovery, the truncated polynomial differs from the original. Cells and proofs generated from this incorrect polynomial would not match the original commitment, causing a silent data corruption.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: Requires demonstrating inconsistent cell inputs that produce nonzero high-degree coefficients after recovery.

properties.text: recover_cells_and_kzg_proofs (SG-024) MUST require at least CELLS_PER_EXT_BLOB / 2 cells (50% threshold). Fewer cells MUST be rejected. Cell indices MUST be unique, sorted in ascending order, and each < CELLS_PER_EXT_BLOB.

reachability.notes: An attacker could provide fewer than 50% cells, duplicate indices, unsorted indices, or out-of-bounds indices to trigger undefined behavior in the recovery algorithm.

properties.severity_justification: Missing the 50% threshold check allows recovery with insufficient data, producing algebraically incorrect results. Missing uniqueness/sorting/bounds checks cause crashes or incorrect recovery.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: Requires PoC demonstrating a client that accepts recovery inputs violating any of these preconditions.

properties.text: After recovery via recover_cells_and_kzg_proofs (SG-024), the regenerated cells and proofs MUST be verifiable against the original KZG commitment using verify_cell_kzg_proof_batch (SG-021). If verification fails, the recovery input cells were inconsistent and MUST be rejected.

reachability.notes: Post-recovery verification is the only way to detect mutually inconsistent input cells. If this step is omitted, an attacker can cause a node to recover and propagate incorrect data.

properties.severity_justification: Missing post-recovery verification allows an attacker to inject inconsistent cells that cause recovery of an incorrect polynomial, which then generates invalid cells and proofs that are propagated as valid data.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: Requires demonstrating a client that does not verify recovered cells against the original commitment.

properties.text: get_validators_custody_requirement (SG-001/validator) MUST always return a value in [VALIDATOR_CUSTODY_REQUIREMENT, NUMBER_OF_CUSTODY_GROUPS]. The clamping min(max(count, VALIDATOR_CUSTODY_REQUIREMENT), NUMBER_OF_CUSTODY_GROUPS) MUST be applied correctly.

reachability.notes: An attacker controlling validator effective_balance (e.g., via minimum deposits) could manipulate custody requirements. Incorrect clamping could allow validators to store zero columns.

properties.severity_justification: If clamping fails and a validator is assigned 0 custody groups, it would not store any columns, reducing network-wide data availability coverage. Coordinated low-stake validators could create coverage gaps.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: Requires demonstrating a validator balance configuration that produces out-of-bounds custody requirement.

properties.text: cells_and_kzg_proofs_construction (SG-002/validator) MUST produce exactly CELLS_PER_EXT_BLOB cell-proof pairs per blob. The proof slicing blobs_bundle.proofs[start:end] MUST be correctly aligned with the cells from compute_cells(blob), where start = i * CELLS_PER_EXT_BLOB and end = (i + 1) * CELLS_PER_EXT_BLOB.

reachability.notes: The blob bundle arrives via the Engine API from the execution layer. If blobs_bundle.proofs length does not match the expected CELLS_PER_EXT_BLOB * len(blobs), proof slicing will be misaligned.

properties.severity_justification: Misaligned cell-proof pairs produce invalid sidecars that are rejected by all peers, effectively causing the proposer to produce a block with unavailable data, potentially missing a slot.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: Requires demonstrating a blob bundle that causes proof misalignment in a client implementation.

properties.text: get_data_column_sidecars (SG-003/validator) MUST produce exactly NUMBER_OF_COLUMNS sidecars, where each sidecar.index == column_index and each sidecar contains exactly one cell and one proof per blob.

reachability.notes: Incorrect sidecar count or incorrect column indexing would cause peers to miss columns or receive duplicates, degrading data availability.

properties.severity_justification: Missing columns or incorrect column indices would prevent peers from collecting enough columns for data recovery, degrading the 50% availability threshold.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: Requires demonstrating incorrect sidecar construction that causes column coverage gaps.

properties.text: get_data_column_sidecars_from_block (SG-004/validator) MUST compute the Merkle inclusion proof targeting the blob_kzg_commitments field at its correct generalized index in the BeaconBlockBody SSZ tree. An incorrect proof target causes all resulting sidecars to fail inclusion proof verification at receiving peers.

reachability.notes: The Merkle proof must target the correct field index for the Fulu fork's BeaconBlockBody. A fork-dependent index that is not updated would produce invalid proofs.

properties.severity_justification: Incorrect Merkle proof causes all sidecars from the proposer to be rejected by the gossip validation layer, making the block's data completely unavailable and wasting the proposer's slot.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: Requires identifying a client where the generalized index for blob_kzg_commitments is incorrect for the Fulu fork.

properties.text: Each sidecar MUST be published to exactly one gossip subnet, deterministically computed as compute_subnet_for_data_column_sidecar(column_index) (SG-006/validator). Publishing to the wrong subnet prevents peers subscribed to the correct subnet from receiving the data.

reachability.notes: Subnet mapping consistency is critical for data availability. If different clients compute different subnet IDs for the same column_index, data would be fragmented across subnets.

properties.severity_justification: Inconsistent subnet mapping across clients causes data column fragmentation, where columns are published to subnets that no peers are subscribed to, degrading data availability.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: Requires demonstrating two clients that compute different subnet IDs for the same column_index.

properties.text: Validators MUST retain data column sidecars for at least MIN_EPOCHS_FOR_DATA_COLUMN_SIDECARS_REQUESTS epochs (SG-007/validator). During this retention period, validators MUST serve sidecars when requested by peers. Premature pruning violates the data availability guarantee.

reachability.notes: An attacker requesting sidecars during the retention period tests whether the validator correctly retains and serves them. Premature pruning would cause the validator to fail serving requests.

properties.severity_justification: Premature sidecar pruning reduces the number of sources for data recovery, potentially causing data unavailability for blocks within the retention window.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: Requires demonstrating a client that prunes sidecars before MIN_EPOCHS_FOR_DATA_COLUMN_SIDECARS_REQUESTS.

properties.text: An adversary submitting a blob with byte sequences encoding values >= BLS_MODULUS MUST be detected and rejected at the bytes_to_bls_field boundary (tb-001). The system MUST NOT silently reduce values modulo BLS_MODULUS, as this would cause implementation-dependent behavior.

reachability.notes: Directly derived from STRIDE tampering threat on tb-001. Adversary controls blob content and can craft byte sequences near or above BLS_MODULUS.

properties.severity_justification: Silent modular reduction vs. rejection across clients produces different field elements from the same blob, causing consensus divergence on polynomial commitments.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: Requires PoC blob with field element encoding >= BLS_MODULUS that behaves differently across clients.

properties.text: The O(n^2) complexity of compute_cells_and_kzg_proofs_polynomialcoeff (SG-019) MUST be bounded such that processing a single blob does not exceed the slot time budget. Rate-limiting or resource allocation mechanisms MUST prevent blob submission from causing denial of service.

reachability.notes: An attacker submitting maximally-sized blobs triggers the O(n^2) proof generation. The FK20 algorithm is recommended for production but may not be implemented in all clients.

properties.severity_justification: O(n^2) proof generation for multiple blobs per block could exceed the slot time budget, causing the validator to miss the slot or produce a late block.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: Requires demonstrating that proof generation for max blobs exceeds the slot time budget in a specific client.

properties.text: If the KZG trusted setup (KZG_SETUP_G1_MONOMIAL, KZG_SETUP_G2_MONOMIAL) is compromised, an adversary possessing the secret trapdoor can forge KZG proofs that validate against arbitrary commitments, breaking all data availability guarantees. The system's security assumption MUST document this single point of failure.

reachability.notes: This is a foundational cryptographic assumption. Exploitation requires compromising the trusted setup ceremony itself, which is outside normal attack surfaces but represents the ultimate security boundary.

properties.severity_justification: Compromised trusted setup allows forging proofs for arbitrary data, completely breaking data availability. This is the single most catastrophic failure mode for the entire PeerDAS system.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: Trusted setup compromise is a foundational assumption outside normal bug bounty scope. However, discovering a weakness in the ceremony protocol would be eligible.

properties.text: The batch verifier (SG-015) MUST use KZG_SETUP_G2_MONOMIAL[n] from the same trusted setup as KZG_SETUP_G1_MONOMIAL. If the G1 and G2 elements are loaded from different setup files or are inconsistent, the verification equation e(LL, LR) == e(RL, [1]) would accept invalid proofs or reject valid ones.

reachability.notes: Requires misconfiguration or file corruption. Runtime consistency checks between G1 and G2 elements could detect mismatches.

properties.severity_justification: G1/G2 mismatch would cause the affected client to produce different verification results than other clients, causing a consensus split.

properties.text: An adversary submitting cells for batch verification (SG-021) with valid formatting but tampered evaluation data that does not correspond to the claimed KZG commitment MUST be rejected by the pairing-based verification equation. The random linear combination via the Fiat-Shamir challenge MUST ensure that tampering with ANY individual cell is detected with overwhelming probability.

reachability.notes: An attacker can submit cells with correct formatting but incorrect evaluations. The batch verification must detect any single tampered cell.

properties.severity_justification: If batch verification fails to detect tampered cells, an attacker can inject arbitrary data that passes verification, completely undermining data integrity.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: Requires PoC with tampered cell evaluations that pass batch verification in a specific client.

properties.text: An adversary MUST NOT be able to reuse a valid proof from one (commitment, cell) pair for a different cell or commitment. The Fiat-Shamir challenge computation (SG-006) MUST include all commitments, cell_indices, cell evaluations, and proofs such that any substitution changes the challenge r and invalidates the verification equation.

reachability.notes: An attacker could attempt to substitute a valid proof for one cell into a verification batch for a different cell. The Fiat-Shamir heuristic must bind all components to prevent such substitution.

properties.severity_justification: Proof substitution would allow an attacker to claim data availability for data they do not have, by reusing proofs from legitimate cells.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: Requires demonstrating a proof substitution that passes batch verification.

properties.text: An adversary providing partial cells for recovery (SG-024) that are individually valid but mutually inconsistent (from different polynomials) MUST be detected via post-recovery verification. The recovery algorithm MUST NOT accept inconsistent cells without a subsequent verification step.

reachability.notes: An attacker can provide cells from different blobs to the recovery function. Without post-recovery verification, the node would recover a wrong polynomial and propagate incorrect data.

properties.severity_justification: Accepting inconsistent recovery inputs without verification allows an attacker to corrupt data availability, causing nodes to disagree on blob contents.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: Requires demonstrating a client that performs recovery without post-recovery commitment verification.

properties.text: A network-level adversary selectively dropping or delaying data column sidecars on specific gossip subnets MUST NOT be able to reduce data availability below the 50% recovery threshold, given the custody group system distributes columns across multiple subnets and multiple validators.

reachability.notes: Network-level censorship of specific subnets is a realistic attack vector. The custody redundancy must be sufficient to survive targeted subnet disruption.

properties.severity_justification: If targeted subnet censorship can reduce column availability below 50%, data recovery fails and the block's data becomes permanently unavailable.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: Requires analysis showing that custody group distribution is insufficient to survive targeted subnet censorship for a specific network topology.

properties.text: An adversary flooding a validator with sidecar requests during the retention period (SG-007) MUST NOT be able to exhaust the validator's resources to the point where it cannot perform its consensus duties. Rate-limiting per-peer MUST be implemented.

reachability.notes: The MUST-serve requirement for sidecars creates a protocol-level amplification vector. Without rate-limiting, an attacker can exhaust validator resources.

properties.severity_justification: DoS via sidecar request flooding could cause a validator to miss attestation or proposal duties, resulting in economic penalties and reduced network participation.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: Requires demonstrating that a specific client's sidecar serving implementation lacks rate-limiting and can be resource-exhausted.

properties.text: By probing which columns a validator serves, an adversary MUST NOT be able to reliably infer the validator's custody group membership. Response behavior (timing, availability) SHOULD NOT differ between custody and non-custody columns.

reachability.notes: An adversary can systematically request all columns from a validator and measure response times or availability to infer custody assignments.

properties.severity_justification: Custody group inference reveals validator identity information across epochs and could enable targeted censorship or eclipse attacks.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: Requires demonstrating a timing or availability side-channel that reliably reveals custody assignments.

properties.text: If the beacon state used for custody requirement computation (SG-001/validator) is forked or stale, incorrect effective_balance values would produce wrong custody assignments. Custody requirements MUST be computed from the finalized or justified state only.

reachability.notes: An attacker could cause temporary forks that present different effective balances, potentially causing custody requirement divergence between nodes.

properties.severity_justification: Fork-based custody manipulation could create coverage gaps where some columns are not held by any validator, making those columns unavailable for recovery.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: Requires demonstrating a fork scenario that causes different custody assignments across clients.

properties.text: During cells_and_kzg_proofs_construction (SG-002/validator), the assertion blobs_bundle.proofs has length CELLS_PER_EXT_BLOB * len(blobs_bundle.blobs) MUST be checked. A blobs_bundle with incorrect proof count MUST be rejected before attempting proof slicing.

reachability.notes: The blobs bundle arrives from the execution layer via Engine API. A malformed bundle could contain too few or too many proofs.

properties.severity_justification: Missing proof count validation would cause out-of-bounds array access during proof slicing, crashing the validator during block production.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: Requires demonstrating a malformed blobs bundle that crashes a client via incorrect proof count.

properties.text: The Merkle inclusion proof in get_data_column_sidecars_from_block (SG-004/validator) MUST target the blob_kzg_commitments field at its correct generalized index in the BeaconBlockBody SSZ tree for the Fulu fork. The generalized index MUST be updated if the SSZ container changes in a fork.

reachability.notes: Fork-dependent generalized index is a common source of cross-client incompatibility. If not updated for Fulu, all sidecars will have invalid Merkle proofs.

properties.severity_justification: Incorrect generalized index causes all sidecars to fail Merkle proof validation, making the entire block's data unavailable and potentially causing a network-wide disruption at fork activation.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: Requires identifying a client with incorrect generalized index for blob_kzg_commitments in the Fulu BeaconBlockBody.

properties.text: If polynomial_eval_to_coeff (SG-007) does not correctly apply the inverse FFT with bit-reversal permutation, the resulting coefficient representation could have higher degree than expected. The quotient polynomial in compute_kzg_proof_multi_impl (SG-014) MUST NOT exceed the trusted setup bounds.

reachability.notes: Every blob submitted triggers polynomial_eval_to_coeff. An incorrect implementation would affect all blobs processed by that client.

properties.severity_justification: Higher-degree polynomials exceed the trusted setup bounds, potentially allowing the proof generator to commit to polynomials outside the supported degree, producing proofs with unknown security properties.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: Requires demonstrating a client where inverse FFT with bit-reversal produces higher-degree coefficients than expected.

properties.text: The recovery operation (SG-023) with O(n log n) FFT operations and element-wise divisions MUST complete within acceptable time bounds even when triggered with the minimum number of cells (CELLS_PER_EXT_BLOB / 2). Implementations MUST rate-limit recovery attempts.

reachability.notes: An attacker can trigger recovery with exactly 50% cells to maximize vanishing polynomial size and thus computational cost.

properties.severity_justification: Repeated recovery with minimum cells could exhaust a node's computational resources, preventing it from keeping up with the chain.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: Requires demonstrating that triggering recovery repeatedly with 50% cells causes resource exhaustion in a specific client.

properties.text: The division step in recover_polynomialcoeff (SG-023) where reconstructed = [a / b for a, b in zip(ez_over_coset, zp_over_coset)] MUST NOT encounter any zero values in zp_over_coset. The coset shift (PRIMITIVE_ROOT_OF_UNITY) algebraically guarantees that the vanishing polynomial evaluates to nonzero on the coset domain.

reachability.notes: If PRIMITIVE_ROOT_OF_UNITY is incorrect, the coset could overlap with the FFT domain, causing zero values in zp_over_coset and division by zero.

properties.severity_justification: Division by zero during recovery crashes the node. If the root of unity is subtly wrong, the crash would only occur for specific missing cell patterns, creating an intermittent DoS vector.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: Requires demonstrating a specific set of missing cells that triggers division by zero in recovery due to incorrect PRIMITIVE_ROOT_OF_UNITY.

properties.text: A validator claiming to have published sidecars but actually withholding them (data withholding attack) MUST be detectable via data availability sampling by multiple independent peers. The economic incentive (staked ETH) MUST align validators with honest behavior.

reachability.notes: Data withholding is a fundamental attack vector against data availability schemes. Detection relies on sampling by honest peers.

properties.severity_justification: Successful data withholding allows invalid state transitions to be finalized if the withheld data contains evidence of invalidity.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: Requires demonstrating a data withholding strategy that evades sampling detection with non-negligible probability.

properties.text: A validator with low effective_balance MUST still be assigned at least VALIDATOR_CUSTODY_REQUIREMENT custody groups. Coordinated low-stake validators MUST NOT collectively reduce the network's data availability coverage for specific columns below the safety threshold.

reachability.notes: An attacker can create many validators with minimum effective_balance to minimize their custody burden while diluting the overall custody coverage.

properties.severity_justification: Requires significant economic investment (many minimum deposits) to meaningfully impact custody coverage. The minimum floor (VALIDATOR_CUSTODY_REQUIREMENT = 8) provides substantial baseline coverage.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: Requires formal analysis showing that a specific number of minimum-stake validators can create a column coverage gap.

properties.text: compute_roots_of_unity MUST produce primitive roots of unity of the correct order for the BLS12-381 scalar field. PRIMITIVE_ROOT_OF_UNITY MUST be a genuine primitive root. Incorrect roots would make all FFT and coset evaluations algebraically meaningless.

reachability.notes: Root of unity is a hardcoded constant. Incorrectness would require a specification error or build-time corruption.

properties.severity_justification: Incorrect primitive root of unity would make ALL polynomial operations algebraically wrong, completely breaking the entire PeerDAS system.

properties.text: bit_reversal_permutation MUST be applied consistently across all code paths: polynomial_eval_to_coeff (SG-007), coset_shift_for_cell (SG-016), coset_for_cell (SG-017), and recover_polynomialcoeff (SG-023). Any inconsistency in permutation application between encoding and decoding paths produces misaligned evaluations.

reachability.notes: Inconsistent bit-reversal between proof generation and verification paths would cause proofs to fail verification only for specific cell indices, creating an intermittent failure pattern.

properties.severity_justification: Inconsistent bit-reversal causes proofs generated by one code path to fail verification through another path, potentially splitting the network between clients that apply BRP differently.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: Requires demonstrating inconsistent bit-reversal application between two code paths in a specific client.

properties.text: The batch verification (SG-015) MUST aggregate resource costs linearly with num_cells: multiple polynomial interpolations, g1_lincomb operations, and a final pairing check. The maximum batch size MUST be bounded to prevent resource exhaustion from a single verification request.

reachability.notes: An attacker can submit very large verification batches to exhaust the verifier's computational resources.

properties.severity_justification: Unbounded batch verification allows an attacker to submit a single request that consumes excessive CPU time, preventing the node from processing other operations.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: Requires demonstrating that a client lacks batch size limits on verification requests.

properties.text: The hash function hash_to_bls_field used in compute_verify_cell_kzg_proof_batch_challenge (SG-006) MUST behave as a random oracle. Specifically, it MUST be computationally infeasible for an adversary to find two different input sets (commitments, cells, proofs) that produce the same challenge r (collision resistance).

reachability.notes: An attacker could attempt to find collisions in the hash function to substitute invalid data that produces the same challenge r as valid data.

properties.severity_justification: Hash collision allows proof substitution attacks where invalid data passes batch verification, completely undermining the cryptographic data integrity guarantee.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: Requires demonstrating a practical collision attack on hash_to_bls_field or identifying use of a weak hash function.

properties.text: The recursive FFT implementation (SG-003) MUST halve the input size at each recursive level, ensuring O(n log n) time complexity. The recursion depth MUST be bounded by log2(len(vals)), preventing stack overflow even for maximum-size inputs (FIELD_ELEMENTS_PER_EXT_BLOB).

reachability.notes: Recursion depth is determined by the input size, which is a protocol constant. Stack overflow would require an implementation bug that does not halve correctly.

properties.severity_justification: Incorrect recursion that does not halve inputs would cause stack overflow or exponential time complexity, crashing the node.

properties.text: get_data_column_sidecars_from_column_sidecar (SG-005/validator) MUST assert len(cells_and_kzg_proofs) == len(sidecar.kzg_commitments). It MUST reuse header, commitments, and inclusion proof from the existing sidecar, ensuring that reconstructed sidecars are identical to originals.

reachability.notes: Used internally for sidecar reconstruction from an existing sidecar. Missing length assertion could cause silent misalignment.

properties.severity_justification: Missing assertion could cause incorrect sidecar reconstruction but the function is used internally with validated inputs.

properties.text: The bilinear pairing check e(LL, LR) == e(RL, [1]) in verify_cell_kzg_proof_batch_impl (SG-015) MUST be sound: it MUST reject with overwhelming probability (1 - 1/q for BLS12-381 scalar field order q) when the underlying KZG polynomial commitment relationship does not hold.

reachability.notes: Soundness of the pairing check is the ultimate cryptographic guarantee. Any implementation error in the pairing library would undermine all data availability.

properties.severity_justification: Unsound pairing check allows forged proofs to pass verification, completely breaking data availability and potentially enabling invalid state transitions to be finalized.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: Requires demonstrating a pairing implementation bug that accepts invalid proofs.

properties.text: The BLS12-381 bilinear Diffie-Hellman assumption MUST hold for the security of all KZG commitments, proofs, and pairing-based verification. The computational hardness of the discrete logarithm problem in BLS12-381 G1/G2 groups MUST be maintained.

reachability.notes: This is a foundational cryptographic assumption. A break would require a mathematical breakthrough in discrete logarithm computation over BLS12-381.

properties.severity_justification: Breaking the discrete logarithm assumption would allow forging all KZG proofs, completely undermining the entire PeerDAS data availability scheme.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: Fundamental cryptographic assumption. A practical DLP break would be a major mathematical breakthrough.

properties.text: The total_node_balance computation in get_validators_custody_requirement (SG-001/validator) MUST be non-negative and MUST correctly sum effective_balance values for all validator_indices. Integer overflow in the sum MUST NOT occur.

reachability.notes: A very large number of validators with high effective_balance could potentially overflow the balance sum in some implementations, causing incorrect custody computation.

properties.severity_justification: Integer overflow in balance sum would produce incorrect custody requirements but requires an extremely large number of high-balance validators.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: Requires demonstrating integer overflow in balance sum for a specific client implementation.
